name: Shi Jian (Linbo)
page_title: Shi Jian (Linbo) 石鑒 (林伯) [Wei, Jin] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Twice sacked for lying about deeds in battle but still gained high rank and respect.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	石鑒 (林伯)


	 Pronunciation 
	Shi2 Jian4 (Lin2ba4)


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Shí Jiàn (Línbà)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Shih Chien (Lin-po)

	 Birthplace 
	from Yanci in Leling (modern-day Yangxin in Shandong)
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Shi Jian, styled Linbo, was from Yanci county, Leling commandery. Although he came from a poor background, he had noble ambitions, and rose through the civil ranks in Wei. In office, he corrected many flaws, probably referring to corruption. This caused the imperial court to fear him.

When Sima Yan founded the Jin dynasty, replacing Wei, he enfeoffed Shi Jian as Viscount of Tangyang. He was sent out to deal with bandits in Qin and Liang provinces, but was removed from his post due to exaggerating or making up lies about his contributions. He was later made Inspector of Yu Province, and during the conquest of Wu by Jin, probably participated. However, he grossly exaggerated his achievements, and was sent back to his hometown by Sima Yan and ordered not to hold office again. Despite this, he kept his viscount title, and in 290, was elevated to Excellency of Works.

When Sima Zhong was reigning, Yang Jun ordered, through an imperial edict, Shi Jian and Zhang Shao to lead troops to put down Sima Liang, whom he saw as a threat because the latter had troops around. Zhang Shao urged Shi Jian to quickly set out so as to carry out those orders, but Shi Jian thought it was a bad idea, prevented Zhang Shao from doing anything, and sent men to secretly watch what Sima Liang did. Sima Liang did nothing but return to Xuchang. He was praised for stopping Yang Jun from doing anything rash. He was soon enfeoffed as County Marquis (or Duke) of Yong’an.

In the Yuankang era, he was made Grand Commandant. He was over eighty years old by then, but he was still imposing and energetic, seeing himself as young still. He soon died on February 12, 294.

His son, Shi Lou, was styled Chujian. Shi Lou succeeded Shi Jian in the peerage but did nothing noteworthy.

full_text:
Shi Jian (Linbo) 石鑒 (林伯)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Shi Jian (石鑒), styledLinbo (林伯), was born in from Yanci in Leling (modern-day Yangxin in Shandong). Twice sacked for lying about deeds in battle but still gained high rank and respect. Shi Jian was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom and the Jin Dynasty. Shi Jian lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
